title: Helictotrichon cantabricum
intro: Helictotrichon cantabricum, also known as Spanish golden grass, is a perennial grass species native to the Iberian Peninsula, prized for its ornamental value and potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Helictotrichon cantabricum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Helictotrichon cantabricum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae. The genus Helictotrichon is part of the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Poeae, which also includes other grasses such as Festuca and Aira. Within the Poeae tribe, Helictotrichon cantabricum is closely related to other species such as Helictotrichon sempervirens and Helictotrichon sibiricum. Helictotrichon cantabricum is often classified as a perennial grass native to mountainous regions of Europe.
